key_insight: Raising cold-spray pressure from 30 to 60 bar increases copper particle velocity, enabling better bonding and a stronger ductile, conductive deposit.
engineering_relevance: For CSAM copper parts, spray pressure is a key process lever to balance electrical conductivity, ductility, and mechanical performance without melting the material.
method: Cold-sprayed copper using a LightSPEE3D system at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar
Measured particle/microstructural changes with XRD and SEM
Evaluated hardness, conductivity, and tensile response
evidence_signal: The study links higher pressure to refined crystallites, higher dislocation density, improved conductivity, and a clear strength-to-ductility transition in tensile testing.
watch_item: Higher pressure improves performance, but the associated hardness drop and possible recovery/recrystallisation effects mean process windows must be tuned for the target application.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
